metadata.credit: Pick order and tier list provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Dan from Draftsim
metadata.rating_system_note: Tier ratings sourced from Draftsim's EOE pick order page (https://draftsim.com/EOE-pick-order.php). The page renders dynamically; ratings below are reconstructed from Draftsim's draft guide analysis (https://draftsim.com/mtg-eoe-draft-guide/), authored by Bryan Hohns (Veveil), and the Draftsim set review (https://draftsim.com/mtg-eoe-limited-set-review/). All data belongs to Draftsim.

tier_key.S: Bomb — Near-game-winning card; take over almost any other card in the pack.
tier_key.A: Exceptional — Strong reason to be in its color; first-pick quality.
tier_key.D: Playable — Acceptable filler; plays a role in specific decks.
tier_key.F: Unplayable / Near-unplayable — Avoid unless no other options.

categories.description: Near-game-winning cards. Take over almost any other card in the pack. First-pick regardless of colors.

cards.name: Mutinous Massacre
cards.note: Near-game-winning sweeper. Gains control of half opponent's creatures, destroys the rest. Pick regardless of color pair.

cards.name: Singularity Rupture
cards.note: Nondiscriminating board wipe. High splash potential in green decks.

cards.name: Beyond the Quiet
cards.note: Devastating nondiscriminating sweeper for blue-based control strategies.

cards.name: Tezzeret, Cruel Captain
cards.note: Premium planeswalker for UB artifacts. Generates value immediately.

cards.name: Biomechan Engineer
cards.note: Best signpost uncommon in the set. Playable even as a splash.

cards.name: Weftwalking
cards.note: Powerful wheel-style effect with high upside in the right deck.

cards.name: Genemorph Imago
cards.note: GU signpost; absurdly powerful when enabled.

categories.description: Powerful cards that are strong reasons to be in their color. Early-to-mid pick priority.

cards.name: Cryogen Relic
cards.note: Best common in the set. Incredible with Selfcraft Mechan, Exosuit Savior, and Adagia.

cards.name: Galactic Wayfarer
cards.note: Top green common. 2-for-1 that creates board presence AND a Lander.

cards.name: Gravkill
cards.note: Premium black removal; pairs perfectly with void payoffs.

cards.name: Codecracker Hound
cards.note: Near-mythic warp card. 2-for-1 with board presence.

cards.name: Gene Pollinator
cards.note: Second-best green common; enables all splashes.

cards.name: Plasma Bolt
cards.note: Efficient removal when void is enabled.

cards.name: Biosynthic Burst
cards.note: Best common pump spell in the format.

cards.name: Knight Luminary
cards.note: Workhorse warp card with strong base stats.

cards.name: Focus Fire
cards.note: Excellent direct damage removal for white.

cards.name: Zero Point Ballad
cards.note: Life-total-limited sweeper; still devastates most boards.

cards.name: Nebula Dragon
cards.note: Top red finisher and ramp payoff.

cards.name: Exosuit Savior
cards.note: Excellent with Cryogen Relic; strong artifact synergy.

cards.name: Tragic Trajectory
cards.note: Top use-case: pair with removal for double void turn.

categories.description: Solid role-players and high-quality inclusions. Good picks mid-draft when your archetype is established.

cards.name: Selfcraft Mechan
cards.note: Key artifact synergy; excellent with Cryogen Relic.

cards.name: Zookeeper Mechan
cards.note: Strong red common; good in multiple archetypes.

cards.name: Auxiliary Boosters
cards.note: Evasive flier; source of inevitability in longer games.

cards.name: Slagdrill Scrapper
cards.note: Excellent void enabler and sacrifice outlet.

cards.name: Illvoi Galeblade
cards.note: Self-sacrifice void enabler with combat stats.

cards.name: Swarm Culler
cards.note: Void enabler and token sacrifice outlet.

cards.name: Rayblade Trooper
cards.note: GW counters signpost with solid body.

cards.name: Starwinder
cards.note: Strong green uncommon with ramp upside.

cards.name: Frenzied Baloth
cards.note: Aggressive bomb; strong in midrange green.

cards.name: Sunset Saboteur
cards.note: Evasive threat with relevant ETB ability.

cards.name: Fungal Colossus
cards.note: Massive landfall/ramp payoff for multi-color builds.

cards.name: Lithobraking
cards.note: Only nonrare sweeper in the set.

cards.name: Pain for All
cards.note: Sweeper with life loss; situationally powerful.

cards.name: Dubious Delicacy
cards.note: Enchantment target for Adagia; value engine.

cards.name: Pinnacle Kill-Ship
cards.note: Spacecraft with removal ability; excellent Adagia target.

categories.description: Acceptable filler and role-players. Include when you need to fill your deck, especially in synergistic builds.

cards.name: Nutrient Block
cards.note: Sacrifice for void; incidental life gain.

cards.name: Cryoshatter
cards.note: Bounce spell; good in two-spell turns with Landers.

cards.name: Survey Mechan
cards.note: Different lands synergy piece for multi-color GU builds.

cards.name: Imodane's Recruiter
cards.note: Key payoff for Kavaron, Memorial World; standalone filler otherwise.

cards.name: Eusocial Engineering
cards.note: Token generator with warp flexibility; enables Lander synergies.

cards.name: Cryogen Relic
cards.note: Duplicate reference — see Great Playables tier.

categories.description: Mediocre cards that require specific conditions or deck archetypes to be worthwhile. Late picks only.

cards.name: Dark Endurance
cards.note: Weak regeneration/protection effect; often a stinker.

cards.name: Gravblade Heavy
cards.note: Too slow for the tempo of the format.

cards.name: Meltstrider's Gear
cards.note: Green's only truly bad card. Avoid.

cards.name: Terminal Velocity
cards.note: Terrible sweeper; rarely worth playing around.

categories.description: Cards that are unplayable or near-unplayable in most circumstances. Do not include unless desperate.

cards.name: Sinister Cryologist
cards.note: Blue's only truly awful card. Warp filler with below-rate body.

rankings.name: Adagia, Windswept Bastion
rankings.note: Best planet in Limited. Copies enchantments when stationed.

rankings.name: Kavaron, Memorial World
rankings.note: Good planet. Unlimited Imodane's Recruiter when stationed.

rankings.name: Susur Secundi, Void Altar
rankings.note: Decent planet. Best in BG with large creatures.

rankings.name: Evendo, Waking Haven
rankings.note: Not worth playing in most decks.

rankings.name: Flight-Deck Coordinator
rankings.note: Gains value from planets and tapped permanents.

rankings.name: Extinguisher Battleship
rankings.note: Board wipe spacecraft; relevant sweeper for go-wide decks.

rankings.name: Honor
rankings.note: Buff spell; useful in counters and go-wide strategies.
